entity_library.dotLumen: A head-mounted device with a black and yellow casing, featuring multiple sensors and a flexible arm, worn by a man walking on a city street, which the narrator describes as "guiding the blind through a crowded world."
entity_library.Edward Chang Lab: A research environment where a woman in a wheelchair is connected to brain-computer interface equipment, with a monitor displaying her face and neural data, including phonetic speech breakdowns, enabling the narrator to describe "giving voice to those who cannot speak," and from which a processed female voice articulates, "To not make me laugh."
entity_library.NVIDIA CorrDiff: A high-resolution atmospheric data visualization system, shown as a swirling storm over Taiwan in vivid purples and oranges, with text indicating 'CorrDiff Atmospheric Data 2km Resolution'. A female speaker describes it as using high-resolution radar-assimilated weather forecasts and reanalysis data, capable of super-resolving extreme events like Typhoon Chanthu from 25 km to 2 km resolution with 1000 times the speed and 3000 times the energy efficiency of conventional weather models. It is also a generative AI model used in ensemble typhoon track predictions, currently optimized for Taiwan, with future global availability for generative supersampling.
entity_library.Galaxy NGC 628: A vibrant spiral galaxy image with orange and blue hues, labeled with scientific credits from Space Telescope Science Institute, Argonne National Laboratory, and PHANGS Team, highlighting 'Dust Enshrouded Stellar Nurseries', which the narrator describes as "illuminating galaxies to witness the birth of stars."
entity_library.Industrial Facility: A large-scale industrial setting with yellow railings, conveyor belts, and machinery, overlaid with the text 'i am a transformer', which the narrator begins to identify as "I am a transformer."
entity_library.Man with Head-Mounted Device: A man in a light blue shirt and jeans, wearing a dotLumen device, walking on a city sidewalk with a green digital grid projected onto the pavement, whose experience is described as "guiding the blind through a crowded world," and who speaks with a slightly processed male voice, saying, "I was thinking about running to the store."
entity_library.Woman in Wheelchair: A woman with short hair, wearing a striped shirt, seated in a wheelchair, connected to brain-computer interface equipment, with a monitor displaying her face and neural data, whose processed voice, generated via the interface, says, "To not make me laugh."
entity_library.Green Gravity xAmplify System: An industrial energy storage system using gravitational potential energy, shown with a crane lifting heavy masses and displaying energy values in kWh, is introduced by a female voice stating, "I am a transformer," and "Harnessing gravity to store renewable power," setting the context for energy transformation and storage.
entity_library.PPPL Fusion Reactor: A tokamak fusion reactor at Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory, visualized with glowing plasma and real-time diagnostics like beam power and instability alerts.
entity_library.Fourier Intelligence Robot: A humanoid robot in a rehabilitation setting, assisting a patient with physical therapy under human supervision, is introduced by a female voice declaring, "I am a trainer," and "Teaching robots to assist," highlighting its role in rehabilitation.
entity_library.Unitree Robot: A bipedal robot in an industrial environment, performing tasks like operating control panels and handling tools, is accompanied by distinct robotic and mechanical sounds, and a female voice stating its purpose "To watch out for danger," emphasizing its role in industrial safety and operation.
entity_library.Moon Surgical Robot: A robotic arm assisting surgeons with precision tasks in an operating room, with a monitor showing a magnified surgical view, is heard with continued robotic and mechanical sounds, and a female voice stating its function "And help save lives," directly linking to its medical application.
entity_library.Iambic Therapeutics NeuralPlexer: A digital visualization of a protein structure, showing a candidate molecule and predicted binding site, is introduced by a female voice stating, "I am a healer," and "Providing a new generation of cures," indicating its role in drug discovery and medical advancement.
entity_library.Hippocratic AI User: A woman in a modern living room, sitting on a couch with a dog, using a tablet for AI-driven health interaction, labeled 'Hippocratic AI', who is heard confirming medical advice during a remote consultation.
entity_library.Wayve AI Driver: A simulated driving system shown from a vehicle’s dashboard perspective, navigating busy metropolitan streets, labeled 'Wayve AI Driver', 'Wayve GAIA-1', and 'Wayve LINGO-1'. A robotic voice, representing the AI, narrates its actions and purpose, accompanied by car horn and traffic noises during the simulation.
entity_library.AI21 Labs: A text generation interface displaying a prompt input field and output suggestions, labeled 'AI21 Labs', where a robotic voice, representing the AI, describes its role in writing and breathing life into words.
entity_library.deepdub: A waveform editing interface with multilingual text overlays, labeled 'deepdub', where a robotic voice, representing the AI, speaks phrases in multiple languages, including 'En muchos idiomas' and 'Y escribí la música'.
entity_library.London Symphony Orchestra: A live orchestra performing in a concert hall, with musicians playing string and brass instruments under a conductor’s direction, labeled 'Performed by London Symphony Orchestra', playing powerful, dramatic orchestral music composed by AIIVA.
entity_library.Wayve GAIA-1: A digital interface displaying a prompt input field with the text 'build busy metropolitan streets,' labeled 'Wayve GAIA-1'.
entity_library.Wayve LINGO-1: A chat interface within the driving simulation, showing AI responses to user queries like 'I am overtaking a stopped bus from the right,' labeled 'Wayve LINGO-1'.
entity_library.Original Music Composed by AI, AIVA: On-screen text crediting AIIVA for composing the original music, arranged by John Paesano, and performed by the London Symphony Orchestra.
entity_library.NVIDIA: The NVIDIA logo, shown in green and white, positioned on the right side of a black screen alongside the SIEMENS logo, representing the company whose Omniverse platform is being connected to Siemens' 'crown jewel accelerator' as part of a new partnership.
entity_library.NVIDIA CEO (Jensen Huang): A man with gray hair, wearing a black textured leather jacket over a black shirt, black pants, and white sneakers, holding a small black remote and gesturing while speaking on a dark stage, his voice clear and enthusiastic with a slight accent as he thanks the audience for attending GTC.
entity_library.Audience: A large, dimly lit crowd seated in an auditorium, bathed in purple lighting, who stand and erupt into loud, enthusiastic applause and cheering as Jensen Huang walks onto the stage, with some individuals raising their hands or recording the event.
entity_library.Stage Setup: A dark, minimalist stage with focused lighting on the speaker, creating a high-contrast environment. The presence of an unseen audience is implied by the initial applause.
entity_library.Timeline Graphic: A green line chart titled '1000X AI Compute in 8 Years,' displaying NVIDIA GPU generations from 2016 (Pascal) to 2024 (Blackwell), with performance metrics like TFLOPS and precision formats (FP16, FP8, FP4), illustrating exponential growth in AI compute power, which Jensen Huang references when stating 'In the last eight years, we've gone 1,000 times.'
entity_library.Technical Diagram on Screen: A green and white diagram titled 'A NEW INDUSTRIAL REVOLUTION' with five components, including 'NIMs', 'NEMO AND NVIDIA AI FOUNDRY', and 'OMNIVERSE AND ISAAC ROBOTICS', displayed on the large screen behind the speaker. These components are explicitly referenced and explained by Jensen Huang in his speech.
entity_library.Isaac Sim: A 3D simulation of a factory with robotic arms moving boxes on conveyor belts, labeled 'Isaac Sim' and 'Techman Robot', demonstrating industrial automation simulation, concluding the segment with a white robot arm manipulating a yellow block in a blue grid environment, and an inset 'NVBLOX Example' showing a hand gesture and its 3D reconstruction, all set to dramatic music and a female voiceover.
entity_library.3D Rendered Stone Cottage: A highly detailed 3D rendering of a stone cottage at dusk, with ivy-covered walls, a green door, flower pots, and warm lighting, credited to Adobe Substance 3D Painter, Blender, and Maxon Zbrush.
entity_library.3D Rendered Classical Sculpture: A monochrome 3D rendering of a classical sculpture depicting multiple figures in dynamic poses, displayed in a gallery setting with arched windows, shown on the stage screen during the presentation.
entity_library.3D Rendered Luxury Watch: A close-up 3D rendering of a luxury watch with a blue face, visible mechanical gears, and a silver casing, credited to Adobe Substance 3D Painter and Autodesk 3ds Max.
entity_library.3D Rendered Circuit Board: A detailed 3D rendering of a complex circuit board with gold and black components, credited to Adobe Substance 3D Designer, Autodesk Maya, BICO Allegro, Foundry Nuke, SideFX Houdini, PTC Creo, and Unity Pixyz.
entity_library.Physics Simulation Interface: A 3D simulation environment showing a robot interacting with objects, with a node-based graph interface and physics settings, labeled 'Warp' and 'PhysX', demonstrating real-time simulation capabilities.
entity_library.Warp: A real-time simulation engine used for rendering dynamic ocean waves, accompanied by a whooshing sound, and high-fidelity 3D materials, shown with a close-up of a textured metallic object.
entity_library.OpenPBR, PhysX: A 3D rendering environment displaying colorful reflective spheres and granular material, accompanied by metallic clanking and grinding sounds, demonstrating advanced material and physics simulation.
entity_library.PhysX Flow: A physics simulation system shown with a knight statue disintegrating with a distinct shattering sound, and a room engulfed in realistic fire with roaring fire sounds, highlighting destruction and fire effects.
entity_library.Siemens STARCCM+ with Omniverse Cloud AP: A CFD simulation visualizing airflow around a white sports car with vibrant color gradients indicating velocity and pressure, displayed on the central stage screen, with the text 'Ansys, Rescale' in the bottom left corner. This visual directly illustrates Jensen Huang's discussion of simulating products in 'full fidelity' and 'completely digitally' as part of accelerating the industry.
entity_library.Cadence Reality Digital Twin Platform: A digital twin of an industrial facility with real-time data overlays and 3D machinery models, underscored by mechanical whirring and clanking industrial sounds, used for operational monitoring.
entity_library.Amazon Robotics: A robotic arm in a kitchen environment placing a pan into an oven, accompanied by robotic arm sounds including a distinct gripper closing, demonstrating real-world robotic applications.
entity_library.Isaac Lab: A simulation environment, described by a female narrator as 'a robot learning application to train Groot on Omniverse Isaac Sim'.
entity_library.NVIDIA NIM - DeepSearch: A warehouse simulation using AI to manage and organize 3D models of barrels, pallets, and forklifts, accompanied by general industrial and warehouse sounds including beeps and machinery, demonstrating automated scene generation.
entity_library.SimReady USD: A 3D simulation environment showing automated placement of cardboard boxes onto pallets in a warehouse, with on-screen text 'Build a SimReady warehouse' and interface elements, accompanied by a complex, rhythmic soundscape of whirring, clanking, and mechanical movements, enhancing the sense of industrial automation.
entity_library.HD Hyundai: A large ship under construction, painted in blue and red with the 'HD Hyundai' logo, shown both in a real-world shipyard and as a detailed 3D digital twin within Siemens Teamcenter X, identified as a 'market leader in sustainable ship manufacturing' that 'builds ammonia and hydrogen-powered ships, often comprising over 7 million discrete parts'.
entity_library.QuadSpinner Gaea, SideFX Houdini: Software tools listed alongside Cadence Reality Digital Twin Platform, suggesting their use in creating the digital twin environment.
entity_library.Agility Robotics, Apptronik, Unitree: Companies associated with the humanoid robot simulation, indicating their involvement in robotics development and training.
entity_library.Rockwell Automation Emulate3D: A simulation tool used alongside Isaac Sim and PowerBI for factory automation and operational efficiency analysis.
entity_library.Siemens Teamcenter X: A software interface used for managing and visualizing 3D models of complex products, introduced as 'our leading product lifecycle management software' from the Siemens Xcelerator platform, used daily by customers to develop and deliver products at scale, and later described as letting companies unify and visualize massive engineering datasets interactively with Omniverse APIs.
entity_library.Audio2Face NIM: A digital interface displaying a 3D human head model being animated by audio input, with real-time facial expression controls and parameters like 'Core Riffage' and 'Emo Tun' visible on the screen.
entity_library.Omniverse Cloud APIs for AV Sim: A simulation of a yellow car driving on a city street, with sensor data overlays including bounding boxes, distance markers, and camera views, is underscored by the continuous, complex mechanical and digital soundscape, suggesting the intricate processing and operation of autonomous vehicle systems.
entity_library.Ansys Perceive EM: A fisheye view of a vehicle’s surroundings in a city environment, with sensor data visualizations and a 360-degree perspective, used for autonomous vehicle perception simulation, is accompanied by the same intricate soundscape, further emphasizing the advanced sensor and processing capabilities.
entity_library.Earth-2: A digital twin of Earth shown from space, with illuminated city lights and atmospheric patterns, highlighting global-scale simulation and data visualization, with a white box zooming in on the storm region over Taiwan. The speaker mentions that generative supersampling will be available as part of the 'NVIDIA Earth-2 inference service' for many regions across the globe.
entity_library.Isaac Perceptor: A component highlighted in the NVIDIA Robotics Platform Adoption diagram, explicitly named and described by Jensen Huang as providing 'incredible state-of-the-art vision odometry, 3D reconstruction, and depth perception' to allow robots to understand their environment.
entity_library.Ansys, Rescale: Text displayed in the bottom left corner of the screen, identifying the simulation software used for the aerodynamic visualization of the white sports car, indicating a collaboration between Ansys and Rescale for high-fidelity simulation, which Jensen Huang highlights as essential 'simulation tools' for product creation.
entity_library.Ansys: The Ansys logo appears in white text with an orange slash, displayed alongside the NVIDIA logo on a black background, indicating a collaborative partnership announced by Jensen Huang to 'CUDA accelerate the Ansys ecosystem' and 'connect Ansys to the Omniverse Digital Twin'.
entity_library.Synopsys: The Synopsys logo appears in white text, displayed alongside the NVIDIA logo, and is later mentioned by Jensen Huang as a key partner in accelerating computational lithography and one of the three companies (alongside Ansys and Cadence) that 'basically build NVIDIA'.
entity_library.Cadence: The Cadence logo is displayed alongside NVIDIA's on the stage backdrop. Jensen Huang extensively discusses Cadence's role in "fluid dynamic simulation," its "Millennium" supercomputer (powered by NVIDIA GPUs), "building Cadence co-pilots," and connecting its "Digital Twin Platform to Omniverse" for accelerating CAE, EDA, and SDA.
entity_library.BLACKWELL Chip Diagram: A close-up technical slide showing the BLACKWELL chip, described by Jensen Huang as 'one giant chip' that pushed beyond the 'limits of physics', with text detailing it as 'THE LARGEST CHIP PHYSICALLY POSSIBLE' with 104 billion transistors, TSMC 4NP process, and 10TB/s NVIDIA High-Bandwidth Interface.
entity_library.BLACKWELL GPU Diagram: A technical slide showing the BLACKWELL GPU as 'THE TWO LARGEST DIES POSSIBLE—UNIFIED AS ONE GPU' with 208 billion transistors and full-cache coherency, featuring a detailed die image, following the initial Blackwell chip diagram.
entity_library.BLACKWELL VERSUS HOPPER Diagram: While not explicitly on screen during this segment, Jensen Huang's speech directly references the comparison by holding up two chips and explicitly naming one as "Hopper," which he describes as "the most advanced GPU in the world in production today," in contrast to the Blackwell chip he also holds.
entity_library.GB200 GRACE BLACKWELL SUPERCHIP: A smaller, square, black circuit board held in Jensen Huang’s right hand, described by him as containing "Blackwell dies connected to a Grace CPU" with a "super fast chip-to-chip link," and part of a "memory coherent" system that allows "this much computation" to fit into a "small of a place."
entity_library.BLACKWELL COMPUTE NODE: The most powerful compute node ever created, with two Grace CPUs and four Blackwell GPUs, delivering 80 petaFLOPS, 1.7TB HBM3e, 32TB/s memory bandwidth, and a liquid-cooled MGX design.
entity_library.CONNECTX-800G INFINIBAND SUPERNIC: Identified with on-screen text, shown as a modular network interface card with multiple ports, emphasizing high-speed data connectivity.
entity_library.BLUEFIELD-3 DPU: A powerful infrastructure processor with line-speed processing of networking, storage, and cybersecurity, and 80GB/s memory bandwidth, shown as a compact hardware module.
entity_library.GB200 NVL72: A stacked server rack design with 18 compute trays, 36 Grace CPUs, and 72 Blackwell GPUs, presented by Jensen Huang as a system that is 'form-fit-function compatible to Hopper' for efficient upgrades, delivering 1.4 exaFLOPS of AI performance, 30TB of HBM3e, and functioning as one giant CUDA GPU.
entity_library.NVLink Switch: A technical component with two NVLink Switch chips, eight ports at 1.8TB/s, and 14.4TB/s aggregate bandwidth, connected via light blue cables, introduced with a distinct "thump" and accompanied by subtle electronic zaps indicating connections.
entity_library.NVLink Switch System: An expanded system with 18 NVLink Switch chips and 72 ports, enabling all-to-all communications at 130TB/s, its evolution accompanied by a powerful hum beginning to build.
entity_library.GB200 Compute Nodes, NVLink Switch and S: A unified system described as 'one massive GPU for generative AI,' with 72 Blackwell GPUs fully connected by NVLink, using copper cabling for 6X less cost and blind-mate connectors for easy installation and serviceability, underscored by a sustained, deep hum.
entity_library.Quantum InfiniBand Switch: A high-throughput switch with 800Gb/s per port and 230.4Tb/s aggregated bidirectional throughput, shown as a pull-out module within a rack amidst continuous mechanical sounds.
entity_library.Quantum-X800 InfiniBand or Spectrum-X800: Multiple switch units forming a network for the highest AI performance, with 14.4 teraFLOPS of in-network computing, shown in a multi-rack configuration.
entity_library.GB200 NVL72 Compute Racks: Eight full-scale compute racks, each containing GB200 NVL72 systems, totaling 288 Grace CPUs and 576 Blackwell GPUs, with 2X reduced rack cooling power, connected via a liquid-cooling system, with a deep, pulsing hum signifying their immense, interconnected compute power.
entity_library.GH100-BB0C-A1 Chip: The second small, square, black chip held by Jensen Huang, identified by him as 'Hopper,' which he states 'changed the world.' It is presented alongside the GB200 chip, with visible text 'GH100-BB0C-A1', and is playfully acknowledged for its past significance as the new 'Blackwell' chip is introduced.
entity_library.GB200 NVL72 Compute Node: A large, square circuit board with a dark gray surface, featuring a central blue chip, two large square components resembling eyes, and intricate circuitry, held by Jensen Huang to showcase its design and scale as a powerful AI compute system, explicitly introduced by him as part of the 'Grace Blackwell system' to enthusiastic applause.
entity_library.Woman in Black Attire: A woman wearing a black long-sleeve top, black pants, and white sneakers, identified by Jensen Huang as 'Janine Paul,' who enters from stage left to hand the larger GB200 NVL72 compute node to Jensen Huang, assisting in the hardware demonstration, and receiving applause and cheers from the audience.
entity_library.On-Screen Text: White text in the top right corner reading 'MARCH 18, 2024', and a purple 'y/ highlights' logo in the bottom right corner, indicating the event date and branding. The date aligns with the narrator's description of the warehouse as a simulation environment.
entity_library.NVLink Switch Chip: A close-up technical slide showing the NVLink Switch Chip with a detailed die image and specifications, which NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang explains is designed to allow 'every single GPU talk to every other GPU at full speed at the same time,' effectively making them 'one giant GPU' by driving copper directly with its 'phenomenal' SerDes.
entity_library.DGX GB200 NVL72: A large server rack system displayed on screen, described as '1 Giant GPU', with performance metrics including '720 PFLOPS' for Training FP8, '1.44 ExaFLOPS' for Inference FP4, '130 TB/sec' for Multi-Node All-to-All, and '260 TB/sec' for Multi-Node All-Reduce, shown with two physical rack units—one with a front panel and one with a transparent side revealing internal components. Jensen Huang further details its 'DGX MVLink spine' achieving '130 terabytes per second' through its chassis, connected by '5,000 NVLink cables' spanning '2 miles', and highlights its power efficiency by contrasting it with the '20,000 watts' or '2 kilowatts' that would be required for optics transceivers alone.
entity_library.H100 and H200 Server Modules: Three open server modules displayed on black pedestals, revealing intricate internal components such as copper heat sinks, circuit boards, and wiring, with 'H100' and 'H200' labels visible. These modules are explicitly identified by Jensen Huang as representing a modern GPU, containing "600,000 parts," being "liquid cooled" with specific temperature parameters (25°C in, 45°C out at 2 liters/second), and weighing "3,000 pounds" as part of a "120 kilowatt" rack, contrasting them with older, smaller GPUs.
entity_library.Wistron: The Wistron logo is shown on a building facade, and a commemorative photo of their N2 Factory's inaugural batch shipment is displayed. A female voice details how the Omniverse Digital Twin helped Wistron bring their factory online faster and achieve 50% reduction in cycle times and 40% reduction in defect rates.
entity_library.Omniverse Digital Twin: A highly detailed 3D simulation of a factory floor with white machinery, conveyor belts, and real-time status indicators, also encompassing a 3D simulation of a vast warehouse with pallets, forklifts, and workers, which a deeper male voice-over, speaking over dramatic orchestral music, describes as where 'AI agents helping robots, workers, and infrastructure navigate unpredictable events in complex industrial spaces will be built and evaluated first in sophisticated digital twins.'
entity_library.Apple Vision Pro: A white head-mounted device shown in a diagram on the screen, connected to the NVIDIA Omniverse Cloud, and explicitly mentioned by Jensen Huang as the device to which Omniverse Cloud streams, enabling immersive virtual experiences and acting as a 'portal'.
entity_library.AI Vision Diagram: A visual flowchart on screen showing a kitten photo on the left, an arrow to a 2012 AlexNet neural network schematic labeled 'FIRST CONTACT', and another arrow to a box labeled 'CAT'. This diagram is directly referenced by Jensen Huang as he explains the foundational moment of AI image recognition and its ability to identify various forms of "cats."
entity_library.Global AI Forecast: A lower-resolution weather forecast visualization, shown on the left side of a split screen, displaying Taiwan with less detail and a purple background, labeled 'Global AI Forecast 25 km resolution', implicitly contrasted by the speaker with CorrDiff's higher resolution capabilities.
entity_library.NVIDIA FourCastNet and CorrDiff Ensemble: A global weather simulation interface showing a central map of East Asia with multiple yellow and white predictive tracks fanning out from a storm near Taiwan, surrounded by a grid of smaller storm simulations, labeled 'NVIDIA FourCastNet and CorrDiff Ensemble of Typhoon Track Predictions'. The speaker explains this involves combining the speed and accuracy of NVIDIA's weather forecasting model FourCastNet and generative AI models like CorrDiff to explore hundreds or thousands of kilometer-scale regional weather forecasts.
entity_library.The Weather Company: The logo displayed on the large screen behind Jensen Huang, featuring white text with a leaf icon, positioned next to the NVIDIA logo to signify a partnership that Jensen Huang describes as 'the trusted source of global weather predictions', with NVIDIA collaborating to 'accelerate their weather simulation' and integrate 'Earth-2 Co-Dev' for 'regional, high-resolution weather prediction'.
entity_library.NVIDIA HEALTHCARE: A presentation slide titled 'NVIDIA HEALTHCARE' featuring four domains: IMAGING & ROBOTICS, GENOMICS, BIOINFORMATICS, and DRUG DISCOVERY, each with 3D visualizations and associated tools like MONAI | Holoscan, Parabricks, RAPIDS, pandas, and BioNeMo, which is later highlighted by a female narrator as part of NVIDIA BioNeMo NIMS, enabling a new generative screening paradigm for drug discovery using tools like AlphaFold, MolMIM, and DiffDock.
entity_library.3D Rendered Molecular Structures: A dynamic 3D grid of molecular structures that evolve over time, visualized alongside 2D and 3D scatter plots with color gradients indicating optimization scores, representing a physics-based simulation optimizing for a molecule's ability to bind to a target protein and other favorable molecular properties in parallel, as explained by a female speaker.
entity_library.NVIDIA BioNeMo: A cloud service for generative AI in drug discovery, shown as a web interface with a 'Protein Folding' tab, where users can input a UniProt ID to generate a 3D protein structure with a prediction score gradient; described by a female speaker as enabling a new paradigm in drug discovery with NIMS, providing on-demand microservices for powerful drug discovery workflows like de novo protein design or guided molecule generation for virtual screening, ultimately helping researchers and developers reinvent computational drug design.
entity_library.Jupyter Notebook Interface: A Python code environment displaying molecular visualization code and a 3D rendering of a protein with a bound molecule, used for running optimization on drug-like chemical datasets.
entity_library.Playground Interface: A user interface for protein folding simulation, allowing input of a UniProt ID to generate a 3D protein model with a color-coded prediction score bar.
entity_library.3D Rendered Protein-Molecule Interaction: A cinematic 3D animation of a molecule binding to a protein, shown in multiple split screens with detailed atomic-level visualization, illustrating the optimization for a molecule's ability to bind to a target protein as mentioned by the speaker.
entity_library.Prompt-Response Interface: A slide displaying a prompt box asking 'What is CTL? Write an example CTL test,' with a detailed response explaining CTL and providing a state machine test example, which Jensen Huang refers to while explaining that 'CTL stands for Compute Trace Library' and that 'it wrote the program.'
entity_library.Chat with PDF Interface: A user interface panel showing a chat window with a document preview, demonstrating the system's ability to interact with PDFs, including a conversation about NVIDIA T4 and H100 performance, which Jensen Huang highlights as an example of using 'multi-modality data' like proprietary company PDFs.
entity_library.SAP: The SAP logo, displayed in blue and white, positioned on a black screen alongside the NVIDIA logo, indicating a partnership announcement. NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang states that 'SAP generates 87% of the world's global commerce' and that 'NVIDIA and SAP are building SAP Joule copilots using NVIDIA NeMo and DGX Cloud.'
entity_library.ServiceNow: The ServiceNow logo with the tagline 'THE INTELLIGENT WORKFLOW COMPANY,' displayed on a green and blue gradient background, detailing AI Research & Development, Engineering & Applied Science, and ServiceNow Global Cloud Services, all powered by NVIDIA since 2018, with references to DGX Cloud, NeMo, TensorRT, and NVIDIA AI Enterprise; Jensen Huang states that 85% of Fortune 500 companies run their people and customer service operations on ServiceNow, using NVIDIA AI Foundry to build 'assist virtual assistants'.
entity_library.Cohesity: The Cohesity logo, displayed on a black background, with a diagram titled 'Cohesity Gala – First to Provide AI-Powered Business Insights from Secondary Data,' showing integration with NVIDIA AI Enterprise, including RAPIDS, cuVS, NeMo Retriever, and NIM Microservices; Jensen Huang explains that Cohesity backs up hundreds of exabytes of data for over 10,000 companies and is working with NVIDIA AI Foundry to build their 'Gaia generative AI agent'.
entity_library.Snowflake: The Snowflake logo, displayed on a black background, with a diagram titled 'DATA + AI – ALL ON NVIDIA,' illustrating applications like Snowflake Copilot and Snowflake Document AI, container services, and enterprise data accelerated by NVIDIA technologies such as NeMo Retriever and Triton Inference; Jensen Huang describes Snowflake as storing the world's digital warehouse in the cloud, serving over 3 billion queries daily for 10,000 enterprise customers, and collaborating with NVIDIA AI Foundry to build co-pilots using NVIDIA NeMo and NIMs.
entity_library.NetApp: The NetApp logo, displayed on a black background, with a diagram titled 'NetApp Unlocks Exabytes of Data for Secure, Private Gen AI,' showing components like NetApp Customer Data, NVIDIA NeMo Retriever, and Gen AI with NetApp, culminating in the phrase 'NETAPP CUSTOMERS CAN NOW TALK TO THEIR DATA'; Jensen Huang highlights that nearly half of the world's files are stored on-prem on NetApp, and NVIDIA AI Foundry is assisting them in building chatbots and co-pilots.
entity_library.Dell AI Factory with NVIDIA: A large diagram titled 'Dell AI Factory with NVIDIA' displayed on screen, labeled as 'INDUSTRY'S FIRST END-TO-END ENTERPRISE AI SOLUTION,' showing layers for Model Lifecycle, Dell Professional Services, NVIDIA AI Enterprise Software, and Dell and NVIDIA Infrastructure, spanning from Edge to Cloud, with logos of Dell Technologies and NVIDIA on either side.
entity_library.Agent Response Interface: A pop-up window with a dark gray background, displaying a query 'Did any unusual situation occur in aisle 3?' and a response 'A shelf collapsed and the boxes fell from shelving at 3:30 PM, leaving boxes blocking the aisle,' along with a live video feed from Camera_048 showing the incident with green bounding boxes around the fallen boxes. This interface is part of the system's ability to handle 'real-world unpredictability' and allows 'operators' to 'ask questions using natural language' with 'generative AI powered Metropolis Vision Foundation models'.
entity_library.Warehouse Workers: Multiple workers wearing safety gear including yellow hard hats, blue vests, and work pants, moving through the warehouse. The narrator refers to these as 'digital workers' or 'AI agents' within the simulation, and mentions 'operators' who interact with the system.
entity_library.Forklifts: Blue and black forklifts navigating the warehouse aisles, lifting and transporting pallets of boxes, with one shown following a green-lit path. entity_library.Multi-Camera Tracking Interface: A split-screen display showing a grid of live camera feeds from various warehouse angles on the left, and a top-down digital map on the right with color-coded dots and numbers tracking 30 individuals in real time.
